title: Integrated Applied Modeling and Communication · Decision Defense
summary: Answer an unfamiliar community planning question from raw requirements through a defensible recommendation. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an independently validated applied-math decision packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Iterate through question, assumptions, variables, model, computation, validation, and communication.
Compare candidate models and choose the simplest one that preserves material behavior.
Change material inputs and assumptions to identify which conclusions remain stable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 20 units, changes by −7 units, then by +4 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Model Cycle evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Model Cycle assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Stakeholder Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend a recommendation with calculations, visual evidence, alternatives, and clear next actions.

checks.description: The decision applies Model Cycle to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Model Cycle.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using defend and recommendation plus specific case evidence.
